response:
The error you're encountering is due to a mismatch between the version of the .NET runtime being used and the version of the WPF assemblies that your application is trying to load. Since you're using .NET 7 for your main application, the runtime should support the latest versions of the .NET libraries. However, the error indicates that the application is attempting to load an older version of the PresentationFramework assembly, which is part of WPF. This can happen if the application is configured to use an older version of the framework or if the necessary assemblies are not properly included in the build output.

One possible cause of this issue is that your WPF project is targeting an older version of the .NET Framework, such as .NET Framework 4.7 or earlier, while your main application is built on .NET 7. This creates a version mismatch because the .NET 7 runtime does not include the older WPF assemblies by default. To resolve this, you should ensure that your WPF project is also targeting .NET 7. This will align the version of the assemblies being used with the runtime, reducing the chances of a version mismatch. Additionally, you should verify that the necessary WPF assemblies are being included in the output directory when the application is built.

Cousin to this issue is the way your application is structured and how the WPF project is integrated into the main application. If the WPF project is a separate solution or a dynamically linked component, it's important to ensure that the correct version of the .NET runtime is being used when the application is executed. On Windows, the application will run under the .NET 7 runtime, but if the WPF project is compiled against an older version of the .NET Framework, it may attempt to load the corresponding assemblies from the GAC (Global Assembly Cache), which might not be available. To prevent this, you should make sure that the WPF project is compiled with the same .NET version as the main application and that all necessary assemblies are included in the output directory.

To ensure that the correct assemblies are available at runtime, you can explicitly reference the correct version of the PresentationFramework assembly in your WPF project. This can be done by editing the project file and specifying the correct version of the assembly. Additionally, you can use the `<AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>` element in the app.config file to allow the runtime to automatically redirect assembly versions.
